reforms.description: Belgium plans to expand regional rental allowance schemes for low-income tenants renting on the private market, making the allowance portable and dependent on local median rents.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve housing affordability, which can enhance economic stability and support low-income households.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect as part of Belgium's housing policy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in housing policy aimed at improving access for low-income tenants.
reforms.source_quote: Expand regional rental allowance schemes for low-income tenants renting on the private market.

reforms.description: Belgium aims to increase subsidies for social rental agencies and make publicly-owned plots available for the construction of affordable housing, notably by the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the construction of affordable housing, the initiative supports the growth of the housing sector and addresses social needs.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been executed as part of Belgium's broader housing strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance affordable housing availability through public-private collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Increase subsidies for social rental agencies and make publicly-owned plots available for construction of affordable housing.
